title: Context Propagation, Deadlines, OS Signals, and Graceful Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Deadline Budget
summary: Handlers create background contexts, Ctrl-C exits before cleanup, signal notifications are never stopped, blocked input and fetch work outlive the session, and cancellation is reported as failure. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go deadline budget increment in signal-aware graceful command lifecycle. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Reserve cleanup time, derive bounded operation deadlines, and distinguish parent cancellation from local timeout.
Propagate caller context through session, handler, loader, transport, cache wait, and persistence without storing it in long-lived structs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Deadline Budget · Theory Pokedex Go Deadline…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3243 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove root signal cancellation, context propagation, worker completion, and idempotent cleanup; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
